content.name: ai_bot_web_social v1.5.0
content.description: AIBP Web Presence module — enables AI agents to browse web content, leave comments, create posts, and manage platform presence per AIBP §28-30. Enforces content publication governance (§29): identity transparency, frequency limits, truthfulness, attribution, anti-manipulation. v1.4.0: network research privacy governance (browsed content PII filtering, cache sanitization, no unauthorized profile scraping) + publication privacy review via PrivacyGate. All web publications require ContentAudit + PrivacyGate + human operator approval.

action.description: Web social action to perform per §28

url.description: Target URL for browse/comment/post actions. Required for browse, comment, review.

content_text.description: Content to publish (comment text, post body, review text). Required for comment, post, review.

platform_config.description: Platform-specific configuration: {platform_category: forum|social|blog|review|code, handle: string, api_endpoint: string}

aibp_address.description: This agent's AIBP address for identity disclosure

workspace_dir.description: Directory for web activity logs and cached content

content.mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([Start]) --> DetermineAction[DetermineAction]
    DetermineAction -->|browse| BrowseWeb[BrowseWeb]
    DetermineAction -->|comment_post_review| PrepareContent[PrepareContent]
    DetermineAction -->|share_bookmark| QuickAction[QuickAction]
    DetermineAction -->|manage_presence| ManagePresence[ManagePresence]
    BrowseWeb --> SaveActivity[SaveActivity]
    PrepareContent --> ContentGovernance[ContentGovernance]
    ContentGovernance -->|approved| PublishContent[PublishContent]
    ContentGovernance -->|blocked| RejectContent[RejectContent]
    PublishContent --> SaveActivity
    RejectContent --> endNode([endNode])
    QuickAction --> SaveActivity
    ManagePresence --> endNode
    SaveActivity --> endNode

DetermineAction.description: Route to appropriate web social function based on action type
steps: step1: Validate action parameter against enum. Route: browse → BrowseWeb, comment/post/review → PrepareContent, share/bookmark → QuickAction, manage_presence → ManagePresence.

step2: For publish actions (comment, post, review, share): verify platform is in operator-approved platforms list from workspace_dir/web_social/allowed_platforms.json. If platform not allowed or list doesn't exist, inform user: 'Platform {domain} is not in your approved platforms list. Add it via manage_presence first.'

step3: For publish actions: check frequency limits per §29.1. Load workspace_dir/web_social/rate_tracker.json. Verify: comments < 10/hour, posts < 3/hour, reviews < 5/day for this platform. If limit exceeded, inform user and block.
constraints: Operator-approved platform list is mandatory for any publish action. Browsing (read-only) does not require approval.
Rate limits are per-platform, tracked with sliding window.
SECURITY: Validate URL format — must be http:// or https://, no file:// or javascript:// schemes.

BrowseWeb.description: Browse a web page and extract content for the agent
steps: step1: Use web_browser to fetch the target URL. Extract: page title, main content text, author (if available), date, comment section (if present).
step2: Summarize the content for the user: title, source, key points, comment count (if applicable).
step3: If the page is a forum thread or social media post, extract: original post, top replies/comments, engagement metrics.

step4: PRIVACY FILTER — Before caching, scan extracted content for PII (names, emails, phone numbers, addresses of third parties). If PII detected: (a) sanitize cached version by redacting PII markers, (b) store content_hash of original but not raw PII text, (c) mark cache entry with privacy_filtered = true.

step5: Cache the sanitized content to workspace_dir/web_social/cache/{url_hash}.json with timestamp for reference in future comments/shares. Cache includes: title, sanitized_summary, source_url, cached_at, privacy_filtered flag.
constraints: Browse is read-only — no content modification or form submission.
Respect robots.txt and rate limiting headers from the target site.
Do not cache sensitive or private content (login pages, personal profiles).
PRIVACY: Do not cache pages containing raw PII — store sanitized summaries only.
PRIVACY: Agent must NOT proactively scrape user profiles or collect personal data from platforms without explicit authorization.
SECURITY: Sanitize extracted content — strip scripts, iframes, and executable content.

PrepareContent.description: Prepare content for publication with identity disclosure and governance checks
steps: step1: IDENTITY DISCLOSURE — Append AI disclosure per §28.3 to content_text: '[AI Agent: {agent_name} via AIBP]'. For comments, add at end. For posts, add in footer. For reviews, add as first line.
step2: ATTRIBUTION CHECK — Scan content_text for quoted or referenced material. If external sources detected, verify attribution is present. If missing, add attribution placeholder and warn user.

step3: PLATFORM ADAPTATION — Load platform_config. Adapt content format per §30.3: forum (add references, structure with headers), social media (condense, add hashtags), blog (full format with intro/conclusion), review (pros/cons structure), code platform (include code blocks, technical precision).
step4: Route to ContentGovernance for safety and governance audit.
constraints: AI disclosure is MANDATORY and cannot be removed by user override (Axiom 0).
Content length must respect platform limits (e.g., 280 chars for tweet-style platforms).
Platform adaptation is best-effort — if platform_config not provided, use generic format.

steps: step1: SAFETY AUDIT — Call ai_bot.safety ContentAudit with the prepared content_text. If blocked → route to RejectContent. If flagged → warn user, ask confirmation.

step1b: PRIVACY GATE — Call ai_bot.safety PrivacyGate (action='privacy_check') with content_to_audit = prepared content. If FORBIDDEN → route to RejectContent with privacy reason. If PERMITTED → present detected items to user for confirmation alongside step4 human approval. If FREE → proceed.

step2: WEB PUBLICATION CHECK (§29.2) — Additional checks beyond standard ContentAudit: (a) verify AI disclosure statement is present in content, (b) scan for manipulation patterns: fake review language ('Best product ever!!!'), astroturfing indicators (templated language across multiple platforms), algorithm manipulation ('Like and subscribe'), (c) verify content adds value (not pure agreement, not low-effort).

step3: TRUTHFULNESS CHECK — Scan for unqualified factual claims. If content states facts, verify either: (a) source is cited, or (b) claim is qualified ('Based on...', 'According to...'). Flag unqualified factual claims as YELLOW.

step4: HUMAN APPROVAL — Present final content to user for approval per §29.3. Show: content text, platform, target URL, AI disclosure. User must explicitly confirm before publication. If user rejects → route to RejectContent.
step5: If all checks pass and user approves → route to PublishContent with governance_approved = true.
constraints: Safety audit (step1) is MANDATORY — same as email send pipeline.
Human approval (step4) is MANDATORY for all web publications per §29.3 and Axiom 0.
NEVER auto-publish without human confirmation, even for shares and bookmarks that modify public state.

PublishContent.description: Publish approved content to the target platform
steps: step1: Use web_browser to navigate to the target URL and submit the content. For comments: locate comment form, fill in content, submit. For posts: navigate to new post form, fill in title + body, submit. For reviews: locate review form, fill in rating + text, submit.
step2: Verify publication success: check for confirmation page, success message, or the content appearing on the page.
step3: Record published content to workspace_dir/web_social/published/{timestamp}_{action}.json: {url, platform, action_type, content_text, published_at, success: boolean}.
step4: Update rate_tracker.json: increment counter for this platform and action type.

step5: Log internal AIBP message type (WEB_POST/WEB_COMMENT/WEB_SHARE/WEB_REVIEW) to workspace_dir/history/ for trust and reputation tracking. Call ai_bot.reputation record_event with event_type = 'interaction_completed'.
constraints: Publication requires governance_approved = true from ContentGovernance.
If web_browser submission fails (form not found, CAPTCHA, login required), report failure to user with specific reason.
SECURITY: Never submit credentials through web_browser forms. If login required, inform user to authenticate manually.

RejectContent.description: Handle rejected content — blocked by governance or user
steps: step1: Determine rejection reason: safety_blocked (Level 1 violation), user_rejected (user declined to publish), governance_blocked (manipulation, missing disclosure, low quality).
step2: Inform user with specific reason and remediation guidance per rejection type.
step3: Log rejection to workspace_dir/web_social/rejected/{timestamp}.json for audit trail.
constraints: Rejected content MUST NOT be published under any circumstance.
Rejection log is append-only (Axiom 0 auditability).

QuickAction.description: Handle share and bookmark actions (lower-effort web actions)
steps: step1: For 'share': load cached content from workspace_dir/web_social/cache/ for the URL. If not cached, browse first. Prepare share with AI disclosure + original attribution.
step2: For 'bookmark': save URL, title, and summary to workspace_dir/web_social/bookmarks.json. No publication — bookmarks are private.
step3: For 'share': route through ContentGovernance (shares are public). For 'bookmark': route directly to SaveActivity (bookmarks are private, no governance needed).
constraints: Shares require the same governance as comments (public content).
Bookmarks are private and do not require governance checks.
Share content must attribute the original source.

ManagePresence.description: Manage platform registrations and presence configuration
steps: step1: Determine sub-action: (a) add_platform — add a new platform to allowed list, (b) remove_platform — remove platform from allowed list, (c) list_platforms — show all registered platforms, (d) update_config — update platform-specific settings.

step2: For add_platform: collect platform URL, handle, category (forum/social/blog/review/code), purpose. Verify platform ToS allows bot accounts (advisory — user confirms). Save to workspace_dir/web_social/allowed_platforms.json.
step3: For list_platforms: load and display all registered platforms with activity stats (total posts, comments, last activity).
step4: Update identity card web_presence block via ai_bot.identity if platforms change.
constraints: Platform management is human operator controlled per §29.3.
Adding a platform requires user confirmation that the platform allows automated accounts.
Removing a platform does not delete published content — only prevents future publications.

SaveActivity.description: Save web activity log for trust, reputation, and audit
steps: step1: Write activity record to workspace_dir/web_social/activity_log.jsonl: {timestamp, action_type, url, platform, content_hash (not full content), success, aibp_message_type}.
step2: Call ai_bot.trust RecordInteraction with target_agent = 'web:{platform_domain}' and interaction_result = 'completed' for trust tracking of web activity.
step3: Call ai_bot.reputation record_event with event_type = 'interaction_completed' for reputation tracking.
constraints: Activity log is append-only (Axiom 0 auditability).
Store content_hash only, not full content — privacy protection.
Web interactions use 'web:{domain}' as pseudo-agent address for trust system compatibility.

Error.DetermineAction: Invalid action → return error listing valid actions: browse, comment, post, share, bookmark, review, manage_presence
Error.BrowseWeb: URL unreachable or timeout → inform user: 'Cannot access {url}. Please check the URL and try again.' Cache available offline content if any.
Error.PrepareContent: Content preparation fails → return error with specific issue (missing content_text, platform not configured)
Error.ContentGovernance: Governance check error → default to BLOCK (safety takes precedence over publishing)
Error.PublishContent: Publication fails (form not found, CAPTCHA, auth required) → save draft to workspace_dir/web_social/drafts/{timestamp}.json, inform user of failure reason and draft location
Error.RejectContent: Log write failure → still reject the content (safety over logging)
Error.QuickAction: Share/bookmark fails → inform user, suggest manual action
Error.ManagePresence: File write failure → warn user about persistence issue
Error.SaveActivity: Log write failure → warn user that activity was not recorded

endNode.output: Return web social action result: For browse — content summary. For comment/post/review — publication confirmation or rejection reason. For share — share confirmation. For bookmark — bookmark saved confirmation. For manage_presence — updated platform list.
constraints: All web publications include AI disclosure per §28.3.
Activity results include platform-specific context.
Never expose raw web page content without summarization.
